steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes Wladyslaw I Herman as 'well-documented' and Lestek with the phrase 'whose historical actuality is disputed.' The correct choice must reflect exactly this contrast between the two figures.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the Gesta provides more evidence for Lestek: the passage says the opposite — Lestek relies on oral tradition, not documented records. ✗
  • Choice B — historians agree Wladyslaw existed but disagree about Lestek's existence: directly matches 'well-documented' vs. 'historical actuality is disputed.' ✓
  • Choice C — Lestek ruled later than Wladyslaw: reverses the timeline — Lestek ruled in the 9th–10th centuries; Wladyslaw ruled 1079–1102. ✗
  • Choice D — both figures are supported by similar stories: the passage distinguishes Lestek's oral tradition from Wladyslaw's documented records. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
